sourceMovie.title: The Lighthouse
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Lighthouse for the same reason it broke through in 2019: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a fantasy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Robert Eggers keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Tideland
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Lighthouse clicked for you, Tideland (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and fantasy, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Shutter Island
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Lighthouse clicked for you, Shutter Island (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Lighthouse?
faq.answer: Try Tideland, Shutter Island, The Witch—each shares DNA with The Lighthouse while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Lighthouse?
faq.answer: The Lighthouse (2019) is best described as Drama, Fantasy, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Lighthouse worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
